{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Online Sales / Telesales Officer (Female)",
  "description": "PC Place is a Nigerian retailer specializing in computers, electronics, and accessories, with both an online store and physical locations. The company&#39;s head office is located in Ikeja, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Online Sales / Telesales Officer&nbsp;(Female)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Manage and respond to customer inquiries via social media, email, website, and messaging platforms (e.g., WhatsApp).
	Promote products/services through online channels including social media, e-commerce platforms, and company website.
	Convert leads into sales by providing accurate product information and persuasive communication.
	Process online orders and ensure timely follow-up and delivery coordination.
	Maintain and update customer database and sales records.
	Monitor online sales performance and prepare periodic reports.
	Identify new market opportunities and strategies to increase online sales.
	Handle customer complaints professionally and ensure customer satisfaction.


Preferred Qualifications


	HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Experience in tech or retail sales is an added advantage.


Requirements:


	Proven experience in online sales, digital marketing, or a similar role.
	Strong communication and negotiation skills.
	Familiarity with social media platforms, e-commerce tools, and CRM systems.
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing strategies.
	Ability to work independently and meet sales targets.
	Good organizational and time management skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;pcplacenigeria@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Online Sales / Telesales Officer (Female) Job at PC Place Nigeria




        
    

        
        PC Place is a Nigerian retailer specializing in computers, electronics, and accessories, with both an online store and physical locations. The company's head office is located in Ikeja, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Online Sales / Telesales Officer (Female)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage and respond to customer inquiries via social media, email, website, and messaging platforms (e.g., WhatsApp).
	Promote products/services through online channels including social media, e-commerce platforms, and company website.
	Convert leads into sales by providing accurate product information and persuasive communication.
	Process online orders and ensure timely follow-up and delivery coordination.
	Maintain and update customer database and sales records.
	Monitor online sales performance and prepare periodic reports.
	Identify new market opportunities and strategies to increase online sales.
	Handle customer complaints professionally and ensure customer satisfaction.


Preferred Qualifications


	HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Experience in tech or retail sales is an added advantage.


Requirements:


	Proven experience in online sales, digital marketing, or a similar role.
	Strong communication and negotiation skills.
	Familiarity with social media platforms, e-commerce tools, and CRM systems.
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing strategies.
	Ability to work independently and meet sales targets.
	Good organizational and time management skills.


Salary
N150,000 - N500,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Executive / Admin Assistant",
  "description": "PC Place is a Nigerian retailer specializing in computers, electronics, and accessories, with both an online store and physical locations. The company&#39;s head office is located in Ikeja, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Customer Service Executive / Admin Assistant

Job Locations:&nbsp;Wuse 2, Wuse 1, Maitama, Utako, and Mabushi - Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
Customer Service Duties:


	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, and in person
	Handle customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions in a timely manner
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions and transactions
	Follow up with customers to ensure satisfaction and retention
	Provide product and service information to customers.


Administrative Duties:


	Manage office correspondence, including emails, calls, and letters
	Maintain filing systems (physical and electronic records)
	Schedule appointments, meetings, and manage calendars
	Prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation
	Monitor office supplies and place orders when necessary.
	Support other departments with administrative tasks as required


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Minimum of an OND / HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration or a related field
	Proven experience in customer service or administrative roles
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	High level of professionalism and attention to detail


Key Skills:


	Customer relationship management
	Problem-solving and conflict resolution
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Communication skills (written and verbal).


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;pcplacenigeria@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Customer Service Executive / Admin Assistant Job at PC Place Nigeria




        
    

        
        PC Place is a Nigerian retailer specializing in computers, electronics, and accessories, with both an online store and physical locations. The company's head office is located in Ikeja, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Executive / Admin Assistant

Job Locations: Wuse 2, Wuse 1, Maitama, Utako, and Mabushi - Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
Customer Service Duties:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Respond promptly to customer inquiries via phone, email, and in person
	Handle customer complaints and provide appropriate solutions in a timely manner
	Maintain accurate records of customer interactions and transactions
	Follow up with customers to ensure satisfaction and retention
	Provide product and service information to customers.


Administrative Duties:


	Manage office correspondence, including emails, calls, and letters
	Maintain filing systems (physical and electronic records)
	Schedule appointments, meetings, and manage calendars
	Prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation
	Monitor office supplies and place orders when necessary.
	Support other departments with administrative tasks as required


Requirements & Qualifications


	Minimum of an OND / HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration or a related field
	Proven experience in customer service or administrative roles
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook)
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team
	High level of professionalism and attention to detail


Key Skills:


	Customer relationship management
	Problem-solving and conflict resolution
	Time management
	Attention to detail
	Communication skills (written and verbal).


Salary
N100,000 - N120,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Manager",
  "description": "The SRS Collection stands as a beacon of premier luxury, health, and wellness hospitality, celebrated for crafting bespoke wellness and lifestyle retreats tailored to discerning business and leisure travelers alike. With three illustrious properties nestled in central Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Inventory Manager with proven experience in nightclub or high-volume hospitality environments.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing inventory control, stock management, and cost efficiency across bar, kitchen, and operational supplies, ensuring accuracy, accountability, and minimal wastage.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and maintain accurate inventory records for beverages, food items, and operational supplies
	Monitor stock levels and ensure timely replenishment to avoid shortages or overstocking
	Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly stock counts and reconciliations
	Track usage patterns and identify discrepancies, shrinkage, or pilferage
	Work closely with bar, kitchen, and procurement teams to ensure efficient stock flow
	Implement and enforce inventory control procedures and best practices
	Analyze inventory data and prepare reports on stock levels, variances, and cost control
	Ensure proper storage conditions and compliance with health and safety standards
	Supervise inventory staff and coordinate stock audits
	Collaborate with management to optimize inventory costs and improve operational efficiency


Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, or a related field
	3&ndash;5 years of proven experience as an Inventory Manager in a nightclub, bar, or high-volume hospitality setting (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of inventory management systems and POS tools
	Excellent numerical and analytical skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, late-night environment
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
	Good communication and team management abilities
	Must have&nbsp;Nightclub Experience


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;srsrecruitment2025@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Inventory Manager Job at SRS Collection




        
    

        
        The SRS Collection stands as a beacon of premier luxury, health, and wellness hospitality, celebrated for crafting bespoke wellness and lifestyle retreats tailored to discerning business and leisure travelers alike. With three illustrious properties nestled in central Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Manager

Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Inventory Manager with proven experience in nightclub or high-volume hospitality environments.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing inventory control, stock management, and cost efficiency across bar, kitchen, and operational supplies, ensuring accuracy, accountability, and minimal wastage.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage and maintain accurate inventory records for beverages, food items, and operational supplies
	Monitor stock levels and ensure timely replenishment to avoid shortages or overstocking
	Conduct daily, weekly, and monthly stock counts and reconciliations
	Track usage patterns and identify discrepancies, shrinkage, or pilferage
	Work closely with bar, kitchen, and procurement teams to ensure efficient stock flow
	Implement and enforce inventory control procedures and best practices
	Analyze inventory data and prepare reports on stock levels, variances, and cost control
	Ensure proper storage conditions and compliance with health and safety standards
	Supervise inventory staff and coordinate stock audits
	Collaborate with management to optimize inventory costs and improve operational efficiency


Requirements


	Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Supply Chain, or a related field
	3–5 years of proven experience as an Inventory Manager in a nightclub, bar, or high-volume hospitality setting (mandatory)
	Strong knowledge of inventory management systems and POS tools
	Excellent numerical and analytical skills
	High level of integrity and attention to detail
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, late-night environment
	Strong organizational and problem-solving skills
	Good communication and team management abilities
	Must have Nightclub Experience


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Office Assistant Job at PC Place Nigeria




        
    

        
        PC Place is a Nigerian retailer specializing in computers, electronics, and accessories, with both an online store and physical locations. The company's head office is located in Ikeja, Lagos.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Office Assistant

Job Locations: Wuse 2, Wuse 1, Maitama, Utako, and Mabushi - Abuja
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Perform general office duties such as filing, photocopying, scanning, and data entry
	Maintain and organize office records, documents, and files
	Handle incoming calls, emails, and correspondence
	Assist in scheduling meetings and managing appointments
	Monitor office supplies and reorder when necessary
	Receive and distribute mails, packages, and deliveries
	Maintain cleanliness and orderliness of the office environment
	Assist with basic bookkeeping and record-keeping tasks
	Support staff and management with administrative tasks as assigned.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Posiiton as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Accountant",
  "description": "Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment &amp; Selection, Performance management, Training &amp; Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:&nbsp;&nbsp;Port Harcourt,&nbsp;Rivers

Job Summary


	The accountant will be responsible for managing financial records, ensuring accuracy in reporting, and supporting the organization&rsquo;s financial health.
	The role involves handling day-to-day accounting tasks, preparing financial statements, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and providing insights to guide management decisions.


Responsibilities


	Maintain full-cycle general ledger, including accounts payable/receivable, bank reconciliations, and month-end closing.
	Prepare and analyze monthly financial statements (P&amp;L, balance sheet, cash flow) with variance explanations specific to leasing, construction, or drilling activities.
	Manage lease agreements (both lessor and lessee perspectives), calculate monthly lease payments, track equipment utilization, and handle buyout.
	Oversee subcontractor and vendor payments, verify work completion, process progress billings, manage lien waivers, and track retention amounts.
	Conduct cost analysis for drilling, lifting, or construction operations. Compare actual and budgeted costs for fuel, maintenance, rig moves, crane rentals, and labor, then provide actionable variance reports to operations managers.
	Handle VAT, sales/use tax, property tax on heavy equipment, and any industry-specific levies (e.g., oil extraction taxes, construction retainage reporting).
	Assist in preparing annual budgets and rolling forecasts for equipment utilization, project margins, and operating expenses.


Requirements


	Must-Have Industry Experience (3+ years in at least one of the following):
	
		Oil and Gas (upstream/midstream services, drilling, or production support).
		Crane leasing or heavy equipment rental company.
		Commercial construction (general contractor or large subcontractor)
	
	
	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Financed, or a related field.
	Familiarity with equipment maintenance costing and lifestyle depreciation; understanding how to distinguish repair expenses from capital improvements and calculate accurate net book value.
	Proficiency with ERP systems or construction/leasing software.
	Ability to communicate financial data to non-finance operations managers (e.g., project managers, fleet supervisors).
	Ability to communicate financial data to non-finance operations managers (e.g., project managers, fleet supervisors).
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.


Salary
N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to:&nbsp;jobscandidate8@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",

  },

Accountant Job at Odixcity Consulting




        
    

        
        Odixcity Consulting is an International Hr consulting and procurement firm. With expertise in Recruitment & Selection, Performance management, Training & Development, Compensation and benefits as well as Outsourcing of goods and services for organizations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant

Job Location:  Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The accountant will be responsible for managing financial records, ensuring accuracy in reporting, and supporting the organization’s financial health.
	The role involves handling day-to-day accounting tasks, preparing financial statements, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and providing insights to guide management decisions.


Responsibilities


	Maintain full-cycle general ledger, including accounts payable/receivable, bank reconciliations, and month-end closing.
	Prepare and analyze monthly financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow) with variance explanations specific to leasing, construction, or drilling activities.
	Manage lease agreements (both lessor and lessee perspectives), calculate monthly lease payments, track equipment utilization, and handle buyout.
	Oversee subcontractor and vendor payments, verify work completion, process progress billings, manage lien waivers, and track retention amounts.
	Conduct cost analysis for drilling, lifting, or construction operations. Compare actual and budgeted costs for fuel, maintenance, rig moves, crane rentals, and labor, then provide actionable variance reports to operations managers.
	Handle VAT, sales/use tax, property tax on heavy equipment, and any industry-specific levies (e.g., oil extraction taxes, construction retainage reporting).
	Assist in preparing annual budgets and rolling forecasts for equipment utilization, project margins, and operating expenses.


Requirements


	Must-Have Industry Experience (3+ years in at least one of the following):
	
		Oil and Gas (upstream/midstream services, drilling, or production support).
		Crane leasing or heavy equipment rental company.
		Commercial construction (general contractor or large subcontractor)
	
	
	A Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Financed, or a related field.
	Familiarity with equipment maintenance costing and lifestyle depreciation; understanding how to distinguish repair expenses from capital improvements and calculate accurate net book value.
	Proficiency with ERP systems or construction/leasing software.
	Ability to communicate financial data to non-finance operations managers (e.g., project managers, fleet supervisors).
	Ability to communicate financial data to non-finance operations managers (e.g., project managers, fleet supervisors).
	Strong attention to detail and problem-solving in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.


Salary
N450,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
